title: Add CKC media album unlink, reorder, and link-edit APIs
operator_request: Capture the CKC album mutation gap found during MT-010: albums can link images, but production album management also needs unlink, ordering, and per-link provenance edits.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Resume by treating album membership as a mutable ordered link table. Do not delete media assets when unlinking album membership.

scope.summary: Add production album management operations so CKC can remove, reorder, and update album media links without losing underlying media assets or notes.
base_scope: Add backend route(s) for unlinking media from an album without deleting the media asset.
Add backend route(s) for reordering album members or setting an explicit album position when the storage model supports it.
Add backend route(s) for updating per-link source_path_ref/source_url_ref and any link-local metadata.
Expose Argus-steerable UI controls for unlink and link provenance edit with clear destructive/non-destructive wording.
Add receipts/statuses that identify actor, album, media asset, and mutation result.
acceptance_criteria: Unlinking a media row removes only the album membership and preserves the media asset plus image notes/tags.
Reordering album members is deterministic and visible after reload.
Editing link provenance updates the album membership occurrence without overwriting global asset provenance unless explicitly requested.
Argus can trigger each mutation and re-inspect the resulting album state.
Manual text explains the difference between unlinking from album and deleting media.
risks_and_controls: Risk: unlink is implemented as asset delete. Control: backend negative test proves asset metadata/notes remain readable after unlink.
Risk: per-link refs overwrite global media provenance. Control: tests use the same asset in two albums with different source refs.
Risk: reorder conflicts under parallel agents. Control: actor-attributed receipts and reload-after-write proof.
verification: Frontend Argus mutation and re-inspection tests.
Manual row coverage for destructive/non-destructive album operations.

handoff.note: Created from MT-010 review finding that current album routes support add/list only.
